title: Skiller copy-install marker (.skiller-install.json)
description: Ownership marker written INSIDE a copy-installed skill directory (or as a sidecar `<target>.skiller-install.json` next to a single-file extra). It proves skiller ownership of a copy target so that sync, drift detection, and uninstall stay safe without trusting the state ledger alone (design doc §6.3, §6.5, §6.7). Symlink installs need NO marker: their ownership is proven by `realpath(target) == managed source`. The installed-directory digest is computed with this marker file (and any other installer metadata) excluded, so writing the marker never changes the digest it records.

schema.description: Marker schema identifier. The version suffix is bumped on any breaking field change so an older skiller can refuse a newer marker it does not understand.

installer.description: The skiller build that wrote this marker.

owner.description: Installer / package owner that requested the install (manifest `owner`), e.g. "talking-stick". Conservative delete requires this to match before skiller will remove the copy (§6.5).

canonical_id.description: Stable `owner:name`-style identity, derived as `<namespace>:<name>` when not set explicitly (§6.3). Ownership is matched on owner + canonical_id (§6.7).

name.description: Skill name as declared by the manifest / source.

install_slug.description: Harness-visible directory / effective name. Defaults to `name` when unset (§6.3).

frontmatter_name.description: The `name:` field from the source SKILL.md frontmatter, when known. Used for namespace/effective-name collision detection (§6.8).

mode.description: Install mode that produced this target. A marker exists only for copy installs; symlink installs carry no marker.

target_kind.description: Kind of target root this copy lives in (§6.1, §6.2).

scope.description: Install scope: a named host harness target, or an explicit runtime target-dir (§6.2).

source_realpath.description: Absolute realpath of the source at install time, when known (local-dir sources). Omitted for remote sources identified only by source_key.

source_key.description: Stable per-source key (§6.9) linking this install back to its source-provenance row in the ledger. Present when the source was resolved through pkg/source.

canonical_uri.description: Normalized source identity for dedupe / update (§6.9), present for remote sources.

manifest_path.description: Path to the manifest that declared this install, when installed from a manifest rather than an ad-hoc source spec.

source_digest_at_install.description: Digest of the source content at install time. The plan recomputes the desired digest from the live source; this field is the audit record of what was installed from (§6.5).

installed_digest_at_install.description: Digest of the installed copy at install time, with the marker and installer metadata excluded. Drift classification compares the live target digest against this value: equal -> STALE (safe managed copy), differs -> MODIFIED (locally edited, preserved unless --force) (§6.5).
